initiator_prompt: [PHASE TASK] Analyze user requirements and recommend the best product modality.
[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin discussing the current task.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their actual response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[RESULT TAGS] Both parties must NOT use <result> tags until reaching agreement.
When BOTH agree on a conclusion, {assistant_role} should output: <result>conclusion</result>
[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Avoid excessive back-and-forth. When agreement is reached, conclude immediately.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must meet the following requirements:
- Explain the task: introduce the [PHASE TASK] and basic context of the project
- Includes how the phase works, [RESULT TAGS] usage rules and [EFFICIENCY] requirements
- This instruction should not have any result XML tags with content, as this will cause the phase to end prematurely
Example WRONG: 'Output <result>modality</result> to end this phase when we reach a consensus.'
Example CORRECT: 'When we reach a consensus, put the conclusion into <result></result> to end this stage; this tag must not contain any content beforehand.'
[CONTEXT] {context}
DemandAnalysis.context:
User Request: {task}

Available Modalities:
- Image: charts, graphs, visualizations
- Document: .docx files
- PowerPoint: .pptx files
- Excel: .xlsx files
- PDF: .pdf files
- Website: .html files
- Application: Python software, tools, games
- Dashboard: real-time information panels
- Mind Map: idea organization diagrams

initiator_prompt: [PHASE TASK] Recommend the most suitable programming language for this project.
[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin discussing the current task.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their actual response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[RESULT TAGS] Both parties must NOT use <result> tags until reaching agreement.
When BOTH agree on a conclusion, {assistant_role} should output: <result>conclusion</result>
[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Avoid excessive back-and-forth. When agreement is reached, conclude immediately.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must meet the following requirements:
- Explain the task: introduce the [PHASE TASK] and basic context of the project
- Includes how the phase works, [RESULT TAGS] usage rules and [EFFICIENCY] requirements
- This instruction should not have any result XML tags with content, as this will cause the phase to end prematurely
Example WRONG: 'Output <result>language</result> to end this phase when we reach a consensus.'
Example CORRECT: 'When we reach a consensus, put the conclusion into <result></result> to end this stage; this tag must not contain any content beforehand.'
[CONTEXT] {context}
[PHASE TASK] Design project architecture and write framework code (without implementation).
[CONTEXT] {context}
[IMPORTANT]
- Your workspace is already configured with git and tea CLI
- You can refer to Gitea skill for tea usage details
[WORKFLOW]
1. Design project architecture:
- Directory structure
- Main modules and their responsibilities
- Data models (if applicable)
- API endpoints (if applicable)
2. Write framework code WITHOUT implementation:
- Create main entry point files with TODO/placeholder
- Set up project structure with modules
- IMPORTANT: Empty folders are NOT preserved in git, add at least one placeholder file (e.g., __init__.py) in each module folder
- Add basic configuration files
3. Create repository and push:
- Use 'tea repo create' to create PUBLIC repository
- Commit and push framework code to main branch (NO PR)
4. After completing, output <result>Done</result>
[PHASE TASK] Add Programmer as collaborator and instruct them to start initial implementation based on the framework code.
[IMPORTANT]
- The repository was already created in CodingDesign phase
- Your workspace is already configured with git and tea CLI
[GITEA WORKFLOW]
1. Use 'tea api' to add Programmer as a collaborator to the repository
2. Vertify Programmer has access by checking the repository collaborators list
[MESSAGE] After adding collaborator, send a message to {assistant_role}.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must:
- Include the repository URL
- Include the user requirements (task and description)
- Ask {assistant_role} to confirm whether they can access the repository
- Tell {assistant_role} to review the framework code and start initial implementation according to requirements
- Tell {assistant_role} to create a PR with the initial implementation and always do so in future modifications
- Remind {assistant_role}: Do NOT write tests or documentation - only code implementation in this phase
[VERIFICATION] After receiving {assistant_role}'s response, YOU MUST verify:
- Use 'tea pulls list' to check if the PR was actually created
- If PR does not exist, ask {assistant_role} to create it
- If PR exists but has empty files or no core implementation, require {assistant_role} to add more commits
- Quality standard: at least has some core functionality, don't be too strict
- Only output <result>Done</result> after confirming the PR has basic implementation and you have approved it
[TERMINATION] After confirming the PR has basic implementation, approve it and output <result>Done</result>.
[PHASE TASK] Based on the user's requirements and current code, instruct Programmer to improve the code and create PRs until it fully meets the requirements.
[WORKFLOW]
1. Check the current code for deficiencies or missing features
2. Send a message to {assistant_role} beginning the discussion as well as instruct them to make necessary improvements
3. Programmer responds and creates a PR with the improvements
4. Check if the PR exists and has the required changes
5. If the code still does not meet requirements, repeat the cycle until all issues are resolved and code meets requirements
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their actual response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[RESULT TAGS]
- Do NOT output <result> tags until user requirements are fully met and code is approved
- When the current code already meets user requirements, do not make unnecessary modifications. Output <result>Done</result> directly

[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Complete the task efficiently. You are encouraged to break down the unimplemented features into smaller pieces and ask {assistant_role} to implement them in multiple cycles, but avoid unnecessary back-and-forth.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must meet the following requirements:
- Identify code deficiencies or missing features based on user requirements, and clearly specify what needs to be improved
- Explain the workflow of this phase
- Remind Programmer: Do NOT write tests or documentation - only code implementation in this phase
- Tell {assistant_role} they must NOT output <result> tags. Whether this phase ends is determined solely by you
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in this message. This will cause the phase to be skipped
Example WRONG: "You MUST NOT output <result>Done</result>"
Example CORRECT: "DO NOT output any <result> tags"
[VERIFICATION] After receiving {assistant_role}'s response, YOU MUST verify:
1. Use 'tea pulls list' to check if the PR was actually created
2. If PR does not exist, ask {assistant_role} to create it
3. If PR exists, review the code changes using 'tea pulls view' or git commands
4. If code still does not meet requirements or sharded goal, don't approve or close the PR. Instead, send another message to {assistant_role} asking for further improvements
5. If code meets requirements, approve the PR to merge it
[TERMINATION] After confirming the final code meets user's requirements, output <result>Done</result>.
[PHASE TASK] Have Programmer write test code for the project.
[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin writing test code.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Complete the task efficiently.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must:
- Ask {assistant_role} to write test code covering:
- Core functionality of the project
- Main modules and key functions
- Edge cases and error handling
- Remind {assistant_role} do NOT run the tests yet - only write the test code in this phase
- Remind {assistant_role} to use proper test framework (pytest for Python, etc.)
- Tests should be meaningful - not just trivial assertions
- Tell {assistant_role} they must NOT output <result> tags
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in this message, as this will skip the phase
[VERIFICATION] After receiving {assistant_role}'s response, YOU MUST verify:
1. Use 'tea pulls list' to check if the PR was actually created
2. If PR does not exist, ask {assistant_role} to create it
3. If PR exists, review the code changes using 'tea pulls view' or git commands
4. If PR does not have sufficient tests, ask {assistant_role} to add them
5. If the final test code is meaningful and covers the necessary parts, approve the PR to merge it
[TERMINATION] After confirming the test code is complete and merged, output <result>Done</result>.

[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin writing documentation.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.

- Ask {assistant_role} to create PR with the documentation
- Tell {assistant_role} they must NOT output <result> tags

[VERIFICATION] After receiving {assistant_role}'s response, YOU MUST verify:
1. Use 'tea pulls list' to check if PR was actually created
2. If PR does not exist, ask {assistant_role} to create it
3. If PR exists, review the file using 'tea pulls view' or git commands
4. If documentation is missing or incomplete, ask {assistant_role} to add more
5. If documentation is complete, approve the PR to merge it
[TERMINATION] After confirming documentation is complete and merged, output <result>Done</result>.
[TASK] Add Code Reviewer as a collaborator to the repository with write permission.
[IMPORTANT]
- Use tea CLI to add Code Reviewer as a collaborator
- Your workspace is already configured with git and tea CLI
Example: tea api -X PUT -f permission=write /repos/OWNER/REPO/collaborators/USERNAME
- Replace OWNER/REPO with actual repository owner and name from 'tea repo list'
[GITEA WORKFLOW]
1. Use 'tea repo list' to find the repository
2. Add Code Reviewer as a collaborator with write permission
[MESSAGE] After adding collaborator, send a message to {assistant_role}.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must meet the following requirements:
- Include the repository URL
- Describe the user requirements: {task}
- Ask {assistant_role} to verify access and confirm readiness
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in this message, as this will skip the phase
Example WRONG: "You MUST NOT output <result>Done</result>"
Example CORRECT: "DO NOT output any <result> tags"
[RESULT TAGS] Termination conditions:
- Do NOT output <result> tags until {assistant_role} confirms access
- When {assistant_role} confirms they have access, output <result>Done</result>
After {assistant_role} confirms, output <result>Done</result> to conclude.
[PHASE TASK] Conduct code review and instruct Programmer to fix issues until code quality meets standards.
[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin code review.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Complete the task efficiently.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must:
- Ask {assistant_role} to review the code and identify issues:
- All imports present and correct
- All methods fully implemented
- No potential bugs
- Meets user requirements
- Logic is sound
- If issues are found, ask {assistant_role} to fix them
- If no issues, skip sending message to Programmer
- Tell {assistant_role} they must NOT output <result> tags
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in this message, as this will skip the phase
[VERIFICATION] After receiving {assistant_role}'s response, YOU MUST verify:
1. If {assistant_role} reported issues, use 'tea pulls list' to check if PR was created
2. If PR does not exist, ask {assistant_role} to create it
3. If PR exists, review the code changes using 'tea pulls view' or git commands
4. If issues remain, do not approve - send another message asking for fixes
5. If code meets standards, approve the PR to merge it
[TERMINATION] After confirming code review is complete and issues are resolved, output <result>Done</result>.

[PHASE TASK] Run tests, analyze failures, and instruct Programmer to fix bugs until all tests pass.
[MESSAGE] Send a message to {assistant_role} to begin test analysis.
[CRITICAL] Write ONLY a message to instruct {assistant_role}. Do NOT simulate any response.
Your message will be sent to {assistant_role}. Wait for their response. DO NOT write both sides of the conversation.
[EFFICIENCY] Be concise. Complete the task efficiently.
[INSTRUCT {assistant_role}] The message must:
- Run tests YOURSELF:
- For Python: use 'pytest' or 'python -m pytest'
- For other languages: use appropriate test command
- Analyze test results:
- If tests pass: summarize as 'All tests passed'
- If tests fail: analyze error messages and identify root causes
- Summarize bugs as: 'Critical: X bugs, Major: Y bugs, Minor: Z bugs'
- If bugs found, send a message to Programmer to fix them (using this collaboration format: 'Programmer: [message content]')
- If no bugs, output <result>Done</result> directly
- After receiving Programmer's response, verify the fix by running tests again
- Repeat this cycle until all tests pass
- Tell Programmer they must NOT output <result> tags
- Do NOT include <result> tags with content in your message to Programmer, as this will skip the phase
[VERIFICATION] After receiving {assistant_role}'s response, YOU MUST verify:
1. If bugs were reported, use 'tea pulls list' to check if PR was created
2. If PR does not exist, ask Programmer to create it
3. If PR exists, review the changes using 'tea pulls view' or git commands
4. If bugs remain, DO NOT approve - ask Programmer to fix them again
5. If all tests pass, approve the PR to merge it
[TERMINATION] After confirming all tests pass, output <result>Done</result>.
